examples.title: Algorithm: Core Scheduling Algorithms
examples.description: Pseudocode for FCFS, SJF, Priority, and Round-Robin scheduling algorithms demonstrating how schedulers select the next thread
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FCFS_Schedule(ready_queue)
INPUT: Queue of threads with arrival time and burst time
OUTPUT: Order of execution and metrics

BEGIN
 ready_queue_sorted ← Sort by arrival time
 current_time ← 0
 total_waiting_time ← 0
 
 FOR each thread IN ready_queue_sorted DO
 IF current_time < thread.arrival_time THEN
 current_time ← thread.arrival_time
 END IF
 thread.start_time ← current_time
 current_time ← current_time + thread.burst_time
 thread.completion_time ← current_time
 thread.turnaround_time ← thread.completion_time - thread.arrival_time
 thread.waiting_time ← thread.turnaround_time - thread.burst_time
 total_waiting_time ← total_waiting_time + thread.waiting_time
 END FOR
 
 RETURN average_waiting_time
END

ALGORITHM SJF_Schedule(ready_queue)
INPUT: Queue of threads with burst times
OUTPUT: Optimal execution order

BEGIN
 current_time ← 0
 completed ← 0
 
 WHILE completed < total_threads DO
 ready_threads ← Filter threads where arrival_time ≤ current_time AND NOT completed
 
 IF ready_threads is empty THEN
 current_time ← Min(uncompleted.arrival_time)
 CONTINUE
 END IF
 
 selected_thread ← Find thread with minimum burst_time in ready_threads
 selected_thread.start_time ← current_time
 current_time ← current_time + selected_thread.burst_time
 selected_thread.completion_time ← current_time
 Mark selected_thread as completed
 END WHILE
END

ALGORITHM RoundRobin_Schedule(ready_queue, time_quantum)
INPUT: Queue of threads, time quantum
OUTPUT: Execution order with context switches

BEGIN
 current_time ← 0
 queue ← ready_queue (circular)
 
 WHILE queue is not empty DO
 current_thread ← Dequeue from front
 
 IF current_thread.remaining_time > time_quantum THEN
 current_time ← current_time + time_quantum
 current_thread.remaining_time ← current_thread.remaining_time - time_quantum
 Enqueue current_thread to rear (preempted)
 PRINT "Context Switch: " + current_thread.name + " preempted"
 ELSE
 current_time ← current_time + current_thread.remaining_time
 current_thread.completion_time ← current_time
 current_thread.remaining_time ← 0
 PRINT "Thread " + current_thread.name + " completed at " + current_time
 END IF
 END WHILE
END

ALGORITHM Priority_Schedule(ready_queue)
INPUT: Queue of threads with priorities
OUTPUT: Execution order based on priority

BEGIN
 current_time ← 0
 completed ← 0
 
 WHILE completed < total_threads DO
 ready_threads ← Filter threads where arrival_time ≤ current_time AND NOT completed
 
 IF ready_threads is empty THEN
 current_time ← Min(uncompleted.arrival_time)
 CONTINUE
 END IF
 
 selected_thread ← Find thread with highest priority (lowest number) in ready_threads
 selected_thread.start_time ← current_time
 current_time ← current_time + selected_thread.burst_time
 Mark selected_thread as completed
 
 // Apply aging: increase priority of waiting threads
 FOR each waiting_thread IN uncompleted DO
 waiting_thread.priority ← waiting_thread.priority - 1
 END FOR
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: Four fundamental scheduling algorithms: FCFS executes threads in arrival order, SJF selects shortest burst first (optimal for waiting time), Round-Robin uses time quantum for fair time-sharing, and Priority scheduling assigns CPU based on thread priority with aging to prevent starvation.

examples.title: Python: Scheduling Algorithms Simulation
examples.description: Complete Python implementation simulating FCFS, SJF, Priority, and Round-Robin with metrics calculation for waiting time, turnaround time, and throughput
examples.code:
"""
Thread Scheduling Algorithms Simulation
Demonstrates FCFS, SJF, Priority, and Round-Robin scheduling
"""

class Thread:
 def __init__(self, tid, arrival, burst, priority=0):
 self.tid = tid # Thread ID
 self.arrival = arrival # Arrival time
 self.burst = burst # Total CPU burst time
 self.priority = priority # Priority (lower = higher priority)
 self.remaining = burst # Remaining time for preemptive algorithms
 self.start = 0 # Start time
 self.completion = 0 # Completion time
 self.waiting = 0 # Waiting time
 self.turnaround = 0 # Turnaround time
 
 def __repr__(self):
 return f"T{self.tid}(A:{self.arrival},B:{self.burst},P:{self.priority})"


def calculate_metrics(threads):
 """Calculate average waiting time and turnaround time"""
 n = len(threads)
 avg_waiting = sum(t.waiting for t in threads) / n
 avg_turnaround = sum(t.turnaround for t in threads) / n
 return avg_waiting, avg_turnaround


def fcfs_scheduling(threads):
 """First-Come, First-Served Scheduling (Non-preemptive)"""
 threads = sorted(threads, key=lambda t: t.arrival) # Sort by arrival
 current_time = 0
 
 for thread in threads:
 if current_time < thread.arrival:
 current_time = thread.arrival
 thread.start = current_time
 current_time += thread.burst
 thread.completion = current_time
 thread.turnaround = thread.completion - thread.arrival
 thread.waiting = thread.turnaround - thread.burst
 
 return threads


def sjf_scheduling(threads):
 """Shortest Job First Scheduling (Non-preemptive)"""
 threads = [t for t in threads] # Copy
 completed = []
 current_time = 0
 n = len(threads)
 
 while len(completed) < n:
 # Find ready threads (arrived and not completed)
 ready = [t for t in threads if t.arrival <= current_time and t not in completed]
 
 if not ready:
 # Jump to next arrival
 next_arrival = min((t.arrival for t in threads if t not in completed))
 current_time = next_arrival
 continue
 
 # Select shortest burst
 selected = min(ready, key=lambda t: t.burst)
 selected.start = current_time
 current_time += selected.burst
 selected.completion = current_time
 selected.turnaround = selected.completion - selected.arrival
 selected.waiting = selected.turnaround - selected.burst
 completed.append(selected)
 
 return threads


def srtf_scheduling(threads):
 """Shortest Remaining Time First (Preemptive SJF)"""
 threads = [t for t in threads]
 completed = []
 current_time = 0
 n = len(threads)
 
 while len(completed) < n:
 ready = [t for t in threads if t.arrival <= current_time and t not in completed]
 
 if not ready:
 next_arrival = min((t.arrival for t in threads if t not in completed))
 current_time = next_arrival
 continue
 
 # Select thread with minimum remaining time
 selected = min(ready, key=lambda t: t.remaining)
 
 # Execute for 1 unit (or remaining if less)
 exec_time = 1
 if selected.remaining <= 1:
 exec_time = selected.remaining
 selected.remaining = 0
 current_time += exec_time
 selected.completion = current_time
 selected.turnaround = selected.completion - selected.arrival
 selected.waiting = selected.turnaround - selected.burst
 completed.append(selected)
 else:
 selected.remaining -= exec_time
 current_time += exec_time
 
 return threads


def priority_scheduling(threads):
 """Priority Scheduling with Aging (Non-preemptive)"""
 threads = [t for t in threads]
 completed = []
 current_time = 0
 n = len(threads)
 
 while len(completed) < n:
 ready = [t for t in threads if t.arrival <= current_time and t not in completed]
 
 if not ready:
 next_arrival = min((t.arrival for t in threads if t not in completed))
 current_time = next_arrival
 continue
 
 # Select highest priority (lowest number)
 selected = min(ready, key=lambda t: t.priority)
 selected.start = current_time
 current_time += selected.burst
 selected.completion = current_time
 selected.turnaround = selected.completion - selected.arrival
 selected.waiting = selected.turnaround - selected.burst
 completed.append(selected)
 
 # Aging: increase priority of waiting threads
 for t in threads:
 if t not in completed and t.arrival <= current_time:
 t.priority = max(1, t.priority - 1) # Increase priority
 
 return threads


def round_robin_scheduling(threads, quantum):
 """Round-Robin Scheduling (Preemptive)"""
 threads = [t for t in threads]
 ready_queue = sorted([t for t in threads if t.arrival == 0], key=lambda t: t.tid)
 current_time = 0
 
 while ready_queue:
 current = ready_queue.pop(0)
 
 if current.remaining > quantum:
 current_time += quantum
 current.remaining -= quantum
 # Add newly arrived threads
 arrived = [t for t in threads if t.arrival <= current_time and t not in ready_queue and t not in completed(threads) and t != current]
 ready_queue.extend(arrived)
 ready_queue.append(current) # Re-add to queue
 else:
 current_time += current.remaining
 current.remaining = 0
 current.completion = current_time
 current.turnaround = current.completion - current.arrival
 current.waiting = current.turnaround - current.burst
 
 return threads


def completed(threads):
 return [t for t in threads if t.remaining == 0]


def print_results(algorithm_name, threads):
 """Print scheduling results"""
 print(f"\n{'='*50}")
 print(f" {algorithm_name}")
 print(f"{'='*50}")
 print(f"{'Thread':<10}{'Arrival':<10}{'Burst':<10}{'Priority':<10}{'Start':<10}{'Completion':<12}{'Waiting':<10}{'Turnaround':<10}")
 print("-"*82)
 
 for t in threads:
 print(f"{t.tid:<10}{t.arrival:<10}{t.burst:<10}{t.priority:<10}{t.start:<10}{t.completion:<12}{t.waiting:<10}{t.turnaround:<10}")
 
 avg_wait, avg_turn = calculate_metrics(threads)
 print(f"\nAverage Waiting Time: {avg_wait:.2f} ms")
 print(f"Average Turnaround Time: {avg_turn:.2f} ms")


# Main simulation
if __name__ == "__main__":
 # Create threads: (tid, arrival, burst, priority)
 thread_data = [
 Thread(1, 0, 24, 2),
 Thread(2, 2, 3, 1),
 Thread(3, 4, 3, 3),
 Thread(4, 6, 10, 2)
 ]
 
 # Run FCFS
 fcfs_threads = [Thread(t.tid, t.arrival, t.burst, t.priority) for t in thread_data]
 fcfs_scheduling(fcfs_threads)
 print_results("First-Come First-Served (FCFS)", fcfs_threads)
 
 # Run SJF
 sjf_threads = [Thread(t.tid, t.arrival, t.burst, t.priority) for t in thread_data]
 sjf_scheduling(sjf_threads)
 print_results("Shortest Job First (SJF)", sjf_threads)
 
 # Run Priority
 pri_threads = [Thread(t.tid, t.arrival, t.burst, t.priority) for t in thread_data]
 priority_scheduling(pri_threads)
 print_results("Priority Scheduling (with Aging)", pri_threads)
 
 # Run Round-Robin
 rr_threads = [Thread(t.tid, t.arrival, t.burst, t.priority) for t in thread_data]
 round_robin_scheduling(rr_threads, quantum=4)
 print_results("Round-Robin (Quantum=4)", rr_threads)
examples.explanation: Python simulation implementing FCFS, SJF, SRTF, Priority, and Round-Robin algorithms. Each algorithm creates thread objects with arrival, burst time, and priority. The code calculates waiting time (time in ready queue), turnaround time (completion - arrival), and demonstrates the trade-offs between algorithms. FCFS suffers from convoy effect while SJF optimizes waiting time but can cause starvation.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n²) for SJF and Priority due to repeated scanning, O(n) for FCFS, O(n×m) for Round-Robin where m is quantum

examples.title: C: Thread Scheduling with Ready Queue
examples.description: C implementation demonstrating thread scheduling with a ready queue using pthreads, context switching simulation, and round-robin scheduling
examples.explanation: C implementation demonstrating thread scheduling with a ready queue. Uses a circular queue data structure to manage the ready queue, implements Round-Robin scheduling with time quantum, shows context switching between threads, and calculates waiting time and turnaround time. The code demonstrates key OS concepts: thread control blocks (TCB), ready queue management, dispatcher functions, and scheduling criteria calculation.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for queue operations, O(n×burst/quantum) for scheduling simulation
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for thread storage and queue
